response.justification: Strong large-scale infrastructure and distributed systems experience (millions of requests/second at Sociomantic, platform automation at Meta, current Google Staff Engineer role) demonstrates technical depth and ability to ship at scale. However, C++ experience falls well short of the 8-year minimum requirement—resume shows only ~6-12 months of professional C++ work, primarily a significant gap for a role explicitly requiring deep C++ expertise. Cloud architecture exposure exists (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) but isn't a primary focus. Worth interviewing if C++ skills exist outside this resume or if the role's C++ needs can be addressed through hiring complementary team members.
strengths: Extensive large-scale infrastructure experience: millions of requests/second at Sociomantic, platform automation handling millions of operations at Meta, Kubernetes deployment expertise
Proven ability to ship products at scale: top 10 US ranking iOS/Apple TV apps, direct mail automation for millions, pricing platform across multiple countries
Current Staff Engineer at Google with full-stack expertise and AI-agent infrastructure contribution, demonstrating senior technical credibility
concerns: C++ experience significantly below minimum requirement: resume shows only ~6-12 months of professional C++ work (National Instruments, Sound Position thesis) versus stated need for 8 years
Cloud architecture focus is secondary: primary background in TypeScript/Node.js, limited explicit cloud platform design work compared to general infrastructure
Limited evidence of formal technical mentoring or leadership in cloud/DevOps contexts: senior titles present but mentoring mostly ad-hoc (Refactoring Dojos) rather than structured team development
